---
format:
  revealjs:
    output-file: bnr_cvd_cases_2023_v1_slides.html
    theme: simple
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    margin: 0.06
    slide-level: 2
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: fade
    controls: true
    progress: true
    center: false
    logo: ../../../../assets/images/uwi-crestonly-20p.png
    footer: "Barbados National Registry | The University of the West Indies"
    css:
      - ../../../../assets/css/reveal-fonts.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
          :root {
            --bnr-navy: #001F3D;
            --bnr-teal: #045174;
            --bnr-sand: #D89C60;
            --bnr-orange: #E87A00;
            --bnr-grey: #5f6368;
            --stroke-purple: rgb(139, 111, 180);
            --heart-red: rgb(164, 22, 26);
          }

          .reveal {
            font-family: "BNR Sans", Arial, sans-serif;
            color: #222;
          }

          .reveal h1,
          .reveal h2,
          .reveal h3 {
            font-family: "BNR Sans", Arial, sans-serif;
            font-weight: 650;
            color: var(--bnr-navy);
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
          }

          .reveal h1 { font-size: 1.95em; }
          .reveal h2 { font-size: 1.45em; margin-bottom: 0.55em; }
          .reveal h3 { font-size: 1.05em; }

          .reveal p,
          .reveal li {
            font-size: 0.78em;
            line-height: 1.25;
          }

          .reveal ul {
            margin-top: 0.2em;
          }

          .title-wrap {
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: 110px 1fr;
            gap: 28px;
            align-items: start;
            margin-top: 70px;
          }

          .title-wrap img {
            width: 96px;
            height: auto;
          }

          .title-heading {
            font-family: "BNR Sans", Arial, sans-serif;
            font-size: 1.95em;
            font-weight: 650;
            color: var(--bnr-navy);
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            line-height: 1.1;
            margin: 0 0 0.35em 0;
          }

          .title-subtitle {
            font-size: 0.86em;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: #222;
            margin-top: 0.15em;
          }

          .deck-kicker {
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            font-size: 0.62em;
            margin-top: 0.9em;
          }

          .deck-rule {
            width: 100%;
            height: 4px;
            background: var(--bnr-sand);
            margin-top: 28px;
          }

          .large-number {
            font-size: 2.2em;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: var(--bnr-navy);
            line-height: 1;
          }

          .label {
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            font-size: 0.56em;
            line-height: 1.15;
            margin-top: 0.25em;
          }

          .number-grid {
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
            gap: 28px;
            margin: 34px 0 20px 0;
          }

          .number-card {
            border-top: 5px solid var(--bnr-sand);
            background: #f7f7f7;
            padding: 20px 18px 18px 18px;
            min-height: 130px;
          }

          .insight-box {
            background: #f7f7f7;
            border-left: 7px solid var(--bnr-sand);
            padding: 20px 24px;
            margin-top: 22px;
          }

          .muted {
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
          }

          .stroke {
            color: var(--stroke-purple);
            font-weight: 700;
          }

          .heart {
            color: var(--heart-red);
            font-weight: 700;
          }

          .figure-title {
            font-size: 1.02em;
            font-weight: 700;
            margin-bottom: 0.55em;
          }

          .figure-note {
            font-size: 0.56em;
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            margin-top: 0.4em;
          }

          .reveal .slide-logo {
            max-height: 54px !important;
          }

          .reveal section img {
            box-shadow: none;
            border: none;
          }

          .audience-citation {
            font-size: 0.82em;
            color: #222;
            line-height: 1.35;
            max-width: 1180px;
            margin-top: 0.8em;
          }

          .briefing-url-label {
            font-size: 0.72em;
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            font-weight: 700;
            margin-top: 1.3em;
            margin-bottom: 0.25em;
          }

          .briefing-url {
            font-size: 0.82em;
            line-height: 1.25;
            color: var(--bnr-teal);
            font-weight: 700;
            word-break: break-word;
            max-width: 1180px;
          }

          .briefing-url a {
            color: var(--bnr-teal);
            text-decoration: underline;
            text-decoration-thickness: 0.08em;
            text-underline-offset: 0.12em;
          }
        </style>
---



::: {.title-wrap}
![](../../../../assets/images/uwi-crestonly-20p.png)

::: {}
<div class="title-heading">Hospital Cardiovascular Cases in Barbados</div>

<div class="title-subtitle">BNR CVD case-count briefing, 2023</div>

::: {.deck-kicker}
Briefing created by the Barbados National Chronic Disease Registry, The University of the West Indies  
Group Contacts • Christina Howitt (BNR lead) • Ian Hambleton (analytics) • Updated on 28 Apr 2026
:::

::: {.deck-rule}
:::
:::
:::

## Key messages

::: {.number-grid}
::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">312</div>
<div class="label">strokes recorded in women in 2023</div>
:::

::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">312</div>
<div class="label">strokes recorded in men in 2023</div>
:::

::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">105</div>
<div class="label">heart attacks recorded in women in 2023</div>
:::

::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">141</div>
<div class="label">heart attacks recorded in men in 2023</div>
:::
:::

::: {.insight-box}
Stroke cases remained above the recent five-year average through much of the year, while heart attacks remained below average for much of the year.
:::

## Why this matters

Counts are the simplest and most transparent way to show what is happening in our hospital.

- Each number represents a person who suffered a heart attack or stroke.
- Together, these numbers show pressure on the health system.
- Before interpreting trends or calculating rates, we need confidence in the raw counts.

::: {.insight-box}
Case counts are the foundation for later surveillance outputs: trends, rates, inequalities, and service-readiness indicators.
:::

## What we did

We reviewed all hospital-registered strokes and heart attacks for 2023 and compared them with the previous five years.

- We examined who was affected: men and women, under 70 and 70 or older.
- We tracked how cases built up week by week.
- We compared 2023 counts with the five-year average to identify when numbers were above or below expected levels.

## <span class="stroke">Stroke</span> and <span class="heart">heart attack</span> cases in 2023 compared with the five-year average

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="68%"}
![](../../../../downloads/files/briefings/cvd_cases_2023_v1/figures/cvd_cases_weekly.png){fig-alt="Cumulative CVD cases in 2023 compared with the 2018 to 2022 average."}
:::

::: {.column width="32%"}
### Main pattern

- Strokes stayed consistently above the five-year average.
- By year end, stroke counts were about 60 cases higher than expected.
- Heart attacks remained below average for much of 2023.

::: {.figure-note}
Figure source: BNR hospital-registered stroke and heart attack cases.
:::
:::
:::

## Interpretation

In 2023, the Barbados National Registry recorded:

- 312 strokes in women and 312 in men.
- 105 heart attacks in women and 141 in men.

::: {.insight-box}
The contrast between stroke and heart attack patterns may reflect how quickly each emergency is recognised and treated, as well as lingering post-COVID changes in health-seeking behaviour.
:::

## What the contrast may suggest

Stroke patients may reach hospital sooner through family or bystander action. People with heart attack symptoms may delay seeking care.

This means hospital activity may shift even when underlying disease burden is also changing.

::: {.insight-box}
Simple counts act as an early warning for changes in both care-seeking behaviour and system readiness.
:::

## Proportion of <span class="stroke">stroke</span> and <span class="heart">heart attack</span> cases occurring before age 70

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="68%"}
![](../../../../downloads/files/briefings/cvd_cases_2023_v1/figures/cvd_cases_age_group.png){fig-alt="Age distribution of hospital-registered stroke and heart attack cases."}
:::

::: {.column width="32%"}
### Premature burden

- Among men, over half of strokes affected those under 70.
- Nearly two-thirds of heart attacks in men occurred before age 70.
- Among women, around 40% of strokes and nearly half of heart attacks occurred before age 70.

::: {.figure-note}
Age threshold: before age 70.
:::
:::
:::

## Premature cardiovascular disease

Across both sexes, a large share of cardiovascular events continue to occur before age 70.

This highlights the ongoing burden of premature cardiovascular disease in Barbados.

::: {.insight-box}
The persistence of high proportions of early events shows that much of Barbados’ cardiovascular disease remains preventable.
:::

## Outputs and citation

Tables, figure data, metadata, and build records are available in the online briefing.

::: {.audience-citation}
Barbados National Registry. *Hospital Cardiovascular Cases in Barbados: BNR CVD case-count briefing, 2023*. Barbados National Chronic Disease Registry, The University of the West Indies. Accessed: [insert date accessed].
:::

::: {.briefing-url-label}
Online briefing
:::

::: {.briefing-url}
[https://uwi-bnr.github.io/info-hub/surveillance/cvd/briefings/case-counts.html](https://uwi-bnr.github.io/info-hub/surveillance/cvd/briefings/case-counts.html)
:::
